content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Both amendments identify real correctness bugs: pending milestone sidecars are only recovered on a later write-lock path and can strand terminal state, and missing-lineage repair for resumed runs currently reuses the original start timestamp, corrupting reconstructed resume timing and derived duration metadata.

## Votes

- `fr-14-aecbd6e5`: Accept
  FsMilestoneSnapshotStore::read_snapshot and FsMilestoneJournalStore::read_journal intentionally mask a torn commit by serving previous_snapshot/previous_journal when only one half of the new state is visible, but recover_pending_state_commit only runs inside with_milestone_write_lock. After a crash between snapshot and journal publication, a terminal milestone may never take another mutating path, so the persisted .state-commit.json can leave readers stuck on the stale pre-terminal state indefinitely even though the sidecar already contains the full next snapshot and journal. That is a concrete durability and recovery defect.
- `fr-14-87a24bda`: Accept
  The resume path durably records run_resumed and calls sync_milestone_bead_start with a fresh resume-time timestamp, but sync_terminal_milestone_task reconstructs missing lineage only from run_started.timestamp. If repair has to call record_bead_start while the milestone is still paused, that timestamp is also used to synthesize the paused->running lifecycle transition, so the reconstructed resume bridge is placed at the original run start instead of the durable resume instant. That in turn skews repaired lineage timing and downstream duration_seconds calculations, so using the durable resume timestamp is the correct fix.
